name: windmill__remote-mcp
description: Official Windmill MCP server, served by every Windmill instance (Cloud or self-hosted) at /api/mcp/gateway. Exposes deployed scripts and flows as individual tools, plus built-in API tools to run and manage scripts, flows, resources, variables, jobs, schedules, and apps.
instructions:
**⚠️ Replace the server URL with your instance's MCP endpoint.** There is no central Windmill MCP server — every Windmill deployment serves its own. The pre-filled URL is a placeholder and will not connect. Use `https://app.windmill.dev/api/mcp/gateway` on Windmill Cloud, or `https://<your-windmill-host>/api/mcp/gateway` on self-hosted. To pin a single workspace, use `https://<your-windmill-host>/api/mcp/w/<workspace-id>/mcp` instead.

**⚠️ Only deployed scripts and flows within the selected scope become tools.** Each deployed script and flow in the connected workspace is exposed as its own tool, on top of the built-in API tools. During setup you can restrict the scope to specific folders or “favorites only” — anything outside that scope, or not yet deployed, will not appear. A missing tool usually means scope or deployment status, not a broken connection.

1. Paste your instance's gateway URL (see above) as the server URL.
2. Connect — no client ID or secret needed. Windmill supports OAuth dynamic client registration (authorization code + PKCE); during authorization you pick the workspace and the tool scope (`mcp:all`, `mcp:favorites`, or per-folder `mcp:scripts:*` / `mcp:flows:*`).
3. Approve — your deployed scripts and flows plus the built-in tools appear.

Prefer token auth? In Windmill, open your **account settings → Tokens**, create a token with **Generate MCP URL**, and paste the resulting URL (`https://<host>/api/mcp/w/<workspace-id>/mcp?token=<token>`) as the server URL — no OAuth step. Treat that URL like an API key: anyone with it can trigger actions in your workspace.

Notes:

- The server uses the HTTP streamable transport.
- Give scripts and flows descriptive titles and descriptions — that is what the model sees when choosing tools.
- `queryDocumentation` is available on Enterprise Edition only.

Docs: https://www.windmill.dev/docs/core_concepts/mcp

tools.name: listScripts
tools.description: List all scripts in the workspace

tools.name: getScriptByPath
tools.description: Get a script by its path

tools.name: deleteScriptByHash
tools.description: Delete a script by hash (admin only)

tools.name: deleteScriptByPath
tools.description: Delete a script at a given path (admin only)

tools.name: runScriptPreviewAndWaitResult
tools.description: Run a script preview and wait for the result

tools.name: getFlowByPath
tools.description: Get a flow by its path

tools.name: getJob
tools.description: Get details of a specific job by ID

tools.name: getJobLogs
tools.description: Get logs for a specific job by ID

tools.name: queryDocumentation
tools.description: Query the Windmill AI documentation assistant (Enterprise Edition only)
